## Freelance Services Agreement (Brand Refresh 2025)

### Preamble

This Freelance Services Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of 2025-01-15 (the "Effective Date") by and between TechStartup Inc., by and through Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director ("Client"), and Chen Creative Studio, by and through Alex Chen, Senior Designer ("Freelancer"). The parties agree as follows.

### Recitals

WHEREAS, Client desires to engage Freelancer to perform a complete visual identity redesign for Client's Project known as "Brand Refresh 2025" including logo, color palette, typography, and a brand guidelines document; and WHEREAS, Freelancer has the skills, experience and expertise to perform the services set forth in this Agreement; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants set forth below, the parties agree as follows.

---

## Clauses (20 total)

### DEFINITIONS

*Type: standard*

1.1 "Services" means the design, development and delivery services to be provided by Freelancer under this Agreement for Brand Refresh 2025, including concept development, design, refinement, consultations, and delivery of assets and documentation described in Section 2.2.

1.2 "Deliverables" means the tangible items to be delivered by Freelancer to Client during the Project as listed in Section 2.2, including but not limited to logo concepts, refined logo artwork, brand guidelines document, and asset packages.

1.3 "Final Deliverables" means the version(s) of Deliverables that: (a) Freelancer delivers as completed Work Product; and (b) Client accepts in accordance with Section 2.5 or that are deemed accepted under Section 2.5.

1.4 "Client Materials" means any materials, information, data, artwork, copy, trademarks, trade names, domain names, proprietary marks, and other content provided by Client to Freelancer for use in connection with the Services.

1.5 "Freelancer Tools" means Freelancer's pre-existing templates, methodologies, processes, software tools, source files (including native design files), fonts, code, techniques, and know-how that Freelancer uses in providing the Services and which are not created specifically for Client as part of the Final Deliverables.

1.6 "Work Product" means all original works of authorship, inventions, concepts, ideas, designs, graphics, logos, documentation and other results developed and delivered by Freelancer specifically for Client under this Agreement, including Final Deliverables but excluding Freelancer Tools, Preliminary Works and any Third-Party Materials.

### SCOPE OF SERVICES; DELIVERABLES; SCHEDULE

*Type: standard*

2.1 Scope. Freelancer will provide the Services described in this Agreement for the Project titled "Brand Refresh 2025," consisting of a complete visual identity redesign including logo, color palette, typography, and a brand guidelines document.

2.2 Deliverables; Due Dates; Fees. The Deliverables, due dates and associated amounts are:
  2.2.1 Logo concepts (3 options) — due January 30, 2025 — $2,000.
  2.2.2 Selected logo refinement — due February 15, 2025 — $1,500.
  2.2.3 Brand guidelines document — due March 1, 2025 — $2,500.
  2.2.4 Asset package delivery — due March 15, 2025 — $2,500.
  2.2.5 Total Project Fee: $8,500.

2.3 Effective Date and Project Deadline. The Effective Date is 2025-01-15. The Project Deadline for delivery of all Deliverables is March 15, 2025.

2.4 Revisions. Client is entitled to up to three (3) rounds of revision per Deliverable as part of the fees. Additional revisions beyond three rounds will be billed at Freelancer's standard hourly rate, subject to prior notice and Client approval.

2.5 Acceptance. Client shall review each Deliverable and provide written acceptance or reasonably specific written comments within seven (7) business days of receipt. If Client does not provide acceptance or comments within seven (7) business days, the Deliverable will be deemed accepted.

### PAYMENT; DEPOSIT; INVOICING

*Type: standard*

3.1 Deposit. Client shall pay a non-refundable deposit of $2,550 (30% of the Total Project Fee) within seven (7) days of executing this Agreement. The deposit will be applied to the Total Project Fee.

3.2 Milestone Payments; Invoicing. Freelancer shall invoice Client for the remainder of the Total Project Fee in accordance with the milestones and amounts set forth in Section 2.2. Each invoice is due and payable within fourteen (14) days of invoice date unless otherwise agreed in writing.

3.3 Payment Method. Payments shall be made by check, ACH, wire transfer, or other mutually agreed method. Client is responsible for any bank fees or transaction costs associated with payment.

3.4 Late Payments. Any amount not paid when due shall accrue interest at the lesser of 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law, plus Freelancer's costs to collect overdue amounts, including reasonable attorneys' fees.

3.5 Allocation of Deposit. The deposit will be credited against invoices issued for the Deliverables. If the deposit exceeds the first invoice amount, the remaining deposit credit will be applied to subsequent invoices.

### KILL FEE; TERMINATION FOR CONVENIENCE

*Type: standard*

4.1 Termination for Convenience by Client. Client may terminate this Agreement for convenience at any time upon written notice to Freelancer.

4.2 Kill Fee. If Client terminates for convenience after Freelancer has commenced performance of the Services, Client shall pay Freelancer a kill fee equal to ten percent (10%) of the unpaid amount of the Total Project Fee at the time of termination, up to a maximum of $850. For clarity, the "unpaid amount" means Total Project Fee minus amounts already paid by Client prior to the effective date of termination.

4.3 Payment of Fees Upon Termination. Upon termination for convenience or for cause, Client shall pay Freelancer for all Services performed and Deliverables provided up to the effective date of termination, including any applicable kill fee under Section 4.2, and any non-cancellable expenses incurred by Freelancer. Payment is due within fourteen (14) days of Freelancer's final invoice.

4.4 Termination for Cause. Either party may terminate this Agreement for material breach by the other party if the breaching party fails to cure the breach within fourteen (14) days after written notice specifying the breach.

### OWNERSHIP; LICENSES; WORK PRODUCT

*Type: standard*

5.1 Ownership of Work Product Upon Payment. Subject to Sections 5.3 and 10.1, upon Freelancer's receipt of full and final payment of all amounts due under this Agreement, Freelancer assigns to Client all right, title and interest in and to the Final Deliverables and Work Product created specifically for Client under this Agreement, including copyrights therein, except for rights reserved under Freelancer Tools and Preliminary Works.

5.2 Freelancer Tools; Reservation of Rights. Freelancer retains ownership of all Freelancer Tools. Freelancer grants Client a perpetual, non-exclusive, worldwide license to use Freelancer Tools only to the extent included in the Final Deliverables and solely as incorporated into those Final Deliverables.

5.3 Third-Party Materials. Any third-party materials or third-party licensed elements used in the Deliverables are subject to the applicable third-party license terms. Client is responsible for any additional licensing fees required by third parties.

5.4 Client Materials. Client retains ownership of its Client Materials. Client grants Freelancer a limited, non-exclusive license to use Client Materials solely to perform the Services under this Agreement.

### MORAL RIGHTS

*Type: standard*

6.1 Waiver of Moral Rights. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Freelancer hereby irrevocably waives and agrees not to assert any moral rights, droit moral, or similar rights in or to the Work Product and Final Deliverables transferred to Client under this Agreement. Where such waiver is not possible under applicable law, Freelancer agrees to provide Client with a written waiver or consent in the fullest extent possible.

6.2 Further Assurances. Freelancer shall execute and deliver any documents and take any actions reasonably requested by Client to effect, perfect or confirm the waivers and assignments described in this Section 6.

### PORTFOLIO USE; PUBLICITY

*Type: standard*

7.1 Portfolio Use. Freelancer may display Final Deliverables and non-confidential Deliverables in Freelancer's portfolio, on Freelancer's website, in marketing materials and in competitions after Client's public launch of the new identity, unless Client notifies Freelancer in writing prior to publication that particular Deliverables are confidential for legitimate business reasons.

7.2 Public Launch. For purposes of this Agreement, "public launch" means the date Client first uses the Final Deliverables publicly in any external communications, marketing, website, or product launch. If there is no public launch within twelve (12) months after delivery of Final Deliverables, Freelancer may use the Deliverables in Freelancer's portfolio unless Client has provided prior written notice of confidentiality per Section 7.1.

7.3 Attribution. Freelancer may include Client's name and a brief description of the Project when using Deliverables in Freelancer's portfolio.

### TRADEMARK DISCLAIMER

*Type: standard*

8.1 Trademark Clearance. Client is solely responsible for clearance, registration, and protection of any trademarks, service marks, trade names, logos, trade dress or other proprietary rights related to the Deliverables.

8.2 No Legal Advice. Freelancer is not a trademark attorney, and nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as legal advice or as a warranty that any Deliverable is free from claims of trademark or other intellectual property infringement. Client should obtain independent legal advice regarding trademark clearance and registration.

### PRELIMINARY WORKS; UNUSED CONCEPTS

*Type: standard*

9.1 Preliminary Works. Preliminary concepts, sketches, mock-ups, mood boards, alternative concepts and other unused or non-finalized materials prepared by Freelancer in the course of performing the Services but not included in the Final Deliverables ("Preliminary Works") remain the sole property of Freelancer and need not be delivered to Client.

9.2 License to Client. Freelancer may, at Freelancer's discretion, grant Client a license to use certain Preliminary Works by separate written agreement and consideration.

9.3 Protection of Freelancer's Work. Client shall not use Preliminary Works in a manner that would misrepresent their status as non-final or that would harm Freelancer's reputation.

### INFRINGEMENT CLAIMS; REMEDIES

*Type: standard*

10.1 Notice of Claim. If any claim, suit or proceeding is brought against Client alleging that any Final Deliverable infringes a third party's intellectual property rights, Client shall promptly notify Freelancer in writing and provide reasonable cooperation and information requested by Freelancer.

10.2 Freelancer Remedies. In the event of such an infringement claim, Freelancer's sole obligation and Client's exclusive remedies shall be, at Freelancer's election and expense, one of the following:
  (i) To procure for Client the right to continue using the affected Final Deliverable; or
  (ii) To replace or modify the affected Final Deliverable so that it becomes non-infringing and substantially equivalent in function and appearance; or
  (iii) If (i) and (ii) are not commercially practicable within a reasonable period, to terminate the license to the affected Final Deliverable and refund Client a prorated portion of the fee paid for that affected Final Deliverable.

10.3 Exclusions. The foregoing remedies do not apply to the extent an infringement claim results from (a) Client's modification of the Final Deliverable not authorized by Freelancer, (b) use of the Final Deliverable in combination with items not provided by Freelancer, or (c) Client Materials supplied by Client.

10.4 Limitation. Freelancer has no liability for infringement claims arising from trademarks or marks provided by Client or chosen by Client without Freelancer's advice on clearance.

### INDEMNIFICATION

*Type: standard*

11.1 Client Indemnity. Client shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless Freelancer and Freelancer's officers, directors, employees and agents from and against any third-party claims, damages, liabilities and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising from (a) Client's use of the Deliverables after delivery (except as permitted by this Agreement), (b) Client Materials, (c) Client's breach of this Agreement, or (d) Client's trademarks or marks.

11.2 Freelancer Indemnity. Freelancer shall defend, indemnify and hold Client harmless from and against third-party claims that the Final Deliverables (excluding Client Materials and Freelancer Tools) infringe such third party's United States intellectual property rights, subject to the remedies set forth in Section 10.2 and the exclusions in Section 10.3.

11.3 Procedure. The indemnified party shall provide prompt written notice of any claim to the indemnifying party, allow the indemnifying party sole control of the defense and settlement of the claim, and reasonably cooperate at the indemnifying party's expense.

### CONFIDENTIALITY

*Type: standard*

12.1 Confidential Information. "Confidential Information" means non-public information designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given its nature and the circumstances of disclosure, including Client Materials and non-public aspects of the Project.

12.2 Obligations. Each party shall (a) keep Confidential Information of the other party confidential using at least the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information but not less than reasonable care, (b) not disclose Confidential Information to any third party except as permitted by this Agreement, and (c) use Confidential Information only to perform its obligations under this Agreement.

12.3 Exceptions. Confidential Information does not include information that (a) is or becomes publicly known through no breach by the receiving party, (b) was rightfully in the receiving party's possession prior to receipt, (c) is rightfully received from a third party without restriction, or (d) is independently developed without use of the disclosing party's Confidential Information.

12.4 Compelled Disclosure. If a party is compelled by law to disclose Confidential Information, that party shall provide the other party with prompt notice and, if permitted, cooperate in seeking a protective order or other remedy.

### WARRANTIES; DISCLAIMERS

*Type: standard*

13.1 Freelancer Warranty. Freelancer warrants that the Final Deliverables will be original to Freelancer and will not knowingly infringe the intellectual property rights of any third party. The warranty in this Section 13.1 is limited to claims brought within one (1) year after delivery of the Final Deliverables and subject to the remedies in Section 10.2.

13.2 Disclaimer. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH IN THIS AGREEMENT, THE SERVICES AND DELIVERABLES ARE PROVIDED "AS IS" AND FREELANCER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.

13.3 No Guarantee of Business Results. Freelancer does not warrant or guarantee any specific business results, market acceptance, increase in sales or other business metrics arising from use of the Deliverables.

### LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

*Type: standard*

14.1 Cap on Liability. EXCEPT FOR A PARTY'S GROSS NEGLIGENCE, WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, OR A PARTY'S INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS HEREUNDER, EACH PARTY'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL AMOUNTS PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT TO FREELANCER UNDER THIS AGREEMENT.

14.2 Exclusion of Consequential Damages. IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, PUNITIVE OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOSS OF PROFITS OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

14.3 Carve-Outs. The limitations in this Section 14 shall not apply to liability arising from (a) a party's gross negligence or willful misconduct, or (b) a party's indemnification obligations under Section 11 for third-party claims.

### ASSIGNMENT

*Type: standard*

15.1 Assignment by Parties. Neither party may assign, delegate or transfer this Agreement or any right or obligation hereunder without the prior written consent of the other party, except that either party may assign this Agreement without consent to an affiliate or to a successor in connection with a merger, consolidation or sale of all or substantially all of its assets or equity (a "Change of Control"). Any purported assignment in violation of this Section 15.1 is void.

15.2 Binding Effect. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective permitted successors and assigns.

### SUBCONTRACTING

*Type: standard*

16.1 Use of Subcontractors. Freelancer may engage qualified subcontractors or contractors to perform portions of the Services. Freelancer shall remain responsible for the performance of its subcontractors and for fulfilling Freelancer's obligations under this Agreement.

16.2 Disclosure. Upon Client's reasonable request, Freelancer will identify subcontractors engaged on the Project, provided that Freelancer may withhold proprietary information regarding subcontractors' identities or rates.

16.3 Payment. Freelancer shall pay all subcontractors and shall remain liable for all amounts due to subcontractors.

### TERMINATION; EFFECTS OF TERMINATION

*Type: standard*

17.1 Termination. This Agreement will terminate upon completion of the Services and payment of all amounts owed, or earlier pursuant to Section 4.

17.2 Effects of Termination. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement for any reason: (a) Client shall pay Freelancer for all Services performed and Deliverables delivered through the effective date of termination (including any applicable kill fee under Section 4.2); (b) Freelancer shall deliver to Client all Final Deliverables for which Freelancer has received full payment; and (c) all licenses and rights granted hereunder shall survive only as expressly stated in this Agreement.

17.3 Survival. Sections 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 17, 19 and 20 and any other provisions that by their nature should survive, shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement.

### NOTICES

*Type: standard*

18.1 Method. All notices required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered by hand, courier, nationally recognized overnight carrier, certified mail (return receipt requested), or email with confirmation of receipt to the addresses set forth below or such other address as a party may designate by written notice.

18.2 Addresses for Notice. Notices to Client shall be to Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director, TechStartup Inc. Notices to Freelancer shall be to Alex Chen, Chen Creative Studio. Either party may update its notice address by written notice to the other party.

### GOVERNING LAW; JURISDICTION

*Type: standard*

19.1 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to conflict of law principles.

19.2 Jurisdiction; Venue. The parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the state and federal courts located in California for any disputes arising out of or related to this Agreement.

### MISCELLANEOUS

*Type: standard*

20.1 Independent Contractor. Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent or partner of Client. Freelancer is solely responsible for all taxes, withholdings and other statutory obligations.

20.2 Force Majeure. Neither party shall be liable for delays or failures to perform caused by events beyond its reasonable control, provided that the affected party promptly notifies the other and uses reasonable efforts to resume performance.

20.3 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties concerning the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings, whether written or oral.

20.4 Amendments. No amendment to this Agreement will be effective unless in writing and signed by both parties.

20.5 Severability. If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

20.6 Counterparts; Electronic Signatures. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts and by electronic signature, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
